panel.basis: Lipid wins both CLEAN independent datasets out-of-sample: Tang per-organ TF 0.52 vs 1.23, Kim grain 0.48 vs monotone 2.05 / W2 1.07 (reliable DF>=15%: 0.20 vs 1.92/1.44). Two datasets, two countries, two endpoint types -> not a Tang artifact.

Lipid is the only variant capturing the Kim grain long-chain RISE that monotone/W2 structurally miss (mono O(0.05) vs obs PFUnDA/PFDoDA ~33-35) -- mechanism-level, consistent with the membrane-partition K_PL gating.

On the CONFOUNDED Li 2025 field data, W2 beats lipid on straw/root TF (0.33 vs 0.57) -- but Li is group-water/surface-confounded and pre-registered as inconclusive (lipid still wins Li grain/root 0.72 vs 1.15/1.47), so this does not bear on the clean-dataset claim.

chief.basis: The lipid-facilitated loading mechanism's out-of-sample generalization is ROBUST across multiple independent datasets, not a Tang artifact: with all params fit on Yamazaki and transferred without refit, lipid wins decisively on BOTH clean datasets (Tang per-organ TF 0.52 vs 1.23; Kim grain 0.48 vs 2.05, reliable 0.20 vs 1.92) and uniquely captures the Kim grain long-chain rise the baselines miss. The only counter-signal is on the pre-registered-as-confounded Li 2025 field data (W2 wins straw/root TF), which does not bear on the clean-dataset claim and where lipid still wins grain/root. Hypothesis supported, with the honest caveats recorded (Li confound; Kim long-chain low-DF). This strengthens hyp-oos-lipid from n=3 (Tang only) to a robust multi-dataset cross-validation.
